column4: Chang et al. 2025 Section 2 and Table 1; commercial N42 sintered NdFeB from Jinshan Magnetic Materials Co., wire-cut to source-described 10 mm x 10 mm x 6 mm cubes, retained as pristine N42 and measured with a NIM 6500C permanent magnetic measuring instrument
column5: Table 1 reports intrinsic coercivity Hcj, remanence Br, and maximum energy product at 25 degC and 120 degC. CGS values are exactly normalized with 1 G=1e-4 T and 1 Oe=1000/(4*pi) A/m. The paper calls the 10 mm x 10 mm x 6 mm pieces cubes despite unequal dimensions. Supplier production lot, composition, original sinter schedule, magnetization protocol, coating state, wire-cut orientation, temperature-control method, field-sweep protocol, repeats, dispersion, and uncertainty are absent. The pristine N42 row did not receive the authors' 800 degC/5 h plus 550 degC/2 h treatment; that distinct state is N42-HT. Figure 10 includes plotted 80 degC curves, but no curve points are digitized or admitted

column4: Chang et al. 2025 Table 2 and printed endpoint-ratio formulas for remanence alpha and intrinsic-coercivity beta between 25 degC and 120 degC
column5: The source prints alpha=-0.129 percent/degC and beta=-0.602 percent/degC. These are normalized to -0.00129 K^-1 and -0.00602 K^-1. Rounded Table 1 endpoints do not reproduce both printed coefficients exactly, so the Table 2 coefficients remain distinct source claims rather than values recomputed from rounded endpoints. They are interval-average coefficients, not continuous constitutive laws, reversible recoil limits, or authorization to extrapolate outside 25-120 degC. No coefficient uncertainty, covariance, irreversible-loss boundary, recoil permeability, or raw demagnetization points are reported
